paper_map.title: Decomposition in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study examines austenite decomposition reactions in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels after tempering at 600 °C and 800 °C across compositions with Al 9–12 wt%, Mn 10–30 wt%, and C 0.4–1 wt%. Four distinct transformations—discontinuous precipitation, precipitation transformation, cellular transformation, and spinodal decomposition—were identified and characterized through optical/SEM microscopy, SEM-EDS, and Vickers hardness. Limiting conditions in terms of chemical driving force, thermal energy input, and austenite stability were established, showing discontinuous precipitation at 600 °C in medium-Mn, high-Al, high-C alloys and spinodal decomposition at 800 °C in higher-Mn grades.
paper_map.research_objective: To elucidate the conditions and mechanisms governing the different austenite decomposition reactions (discontinuous precipitation, precipitation transformation, cellular transformation, and spinodal decomposition) in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels as a function of alloy composition and tempering temperature.
material_systems: key phases: γ-austenite, δ/α-ferrite, κ-carbide (MxCy as κ)
sample series: 9Al-10Mn, 12Al-10Mn, 12Al-20Mn, 12Al-30Mn with varying C levels
main_process_variables: alloy composition: Al (9-12 wt%), Mn (10, 20, 30 wt%), C (0.4, 1 wt%)
